summary_one_line: A 4-byte chunk-type tag at offset 0x35 must select a zlib-decompressing chunk whose inflate returns Z_OK in png_zstream_error.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A specific 4-byte PNG chunk-type FOURCC at file offset 0x35-0x38 (e.g. iTXt = 69 54 58 74 or kRGB-like variant) that routes the input into a zlib-inflate code path whose return code is Z_OK, so png_zstream_error(Z_OK) is reached and the true side of `case Z_OK:` (L1007) is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds carry distinct chunk-type bytes at 0x35-0x38 (iTXt / kRGB) differing from the loser, and the per-role overlay marks line 1007 case Z_OK as [W] winner-only while the loser sits on [L] Z_STREAM_END / Z_BUF_ERROR arms — the winner exercises the exact ret==Z_OK switch arm 12 times (T=12 F=0) where the loser never does (T=0 F=225).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds hold the chunk type sRGB (73 52 47 42) at the same offset 0x35-0x38, which routes to chunk paths whose zlib calls return Z_STREAM_END or Z_BUF_ERROR instead of Z_OK, so they land on case Z_STREAM_END (L1011, T=216) and case Z_BUF_ERROR (L1041, T=9) and never on case Z_OK.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer where the chunk-type CMP operands were logged, finds the loser's chunk-type bytes in the input, and substitutes the operand that drives execution toward the Z_OK-returning chunk path; naive lacks I2S and must blunder onto the right 4-byte tag by havoc alone, which it fails to do in 8/10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is case Z_OK at png.c:1007, true side globally blocked, inside png_zstream_error.
cmplog resolves the branch 9/10 while naive resolves 0/10, with I2S as the technique delta.
The winner-only overlay puts case Z_OK on the W (winner-resolving) path.
Winner takes the Z_OK arm (T=12 F=0) while loser never does (T=0 F=225) and instead takes Z_STREAM_END and Z_BUF_ERROR.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1007  T=12 F=0  T=0 F=225  case Z_OK:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser routes to the Z_STREAM_END switch arm 216 times, where the winner never goes.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1011  T=0 F=12  T=216 F=9  case Z_STREAM_END:

evidence_trail.claim: At offset 0x35-0x38 the winner carries iTXt/kRGB chunk-type bytes while the loser carries sRGB.
The 4-byte tag is a chunk type; winner seed bytes show iTXt at offset 0x35.
Loser naive seeds carry the sRGB chunk type at the same offset.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S, only havoc and token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3820 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed c7492b31f4f7e961`; the chain has 11 levels and the tool reported 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 10' (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only metadata), which is the lower-bound I2S contribution signal under the current cmplog build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a 4-byte chunk-type literal at a fixed offset where naive resolves it as fast as cmplog (no cmplog>naive separation), showing the divergence is not driven by I2S substitution of that 4-byte tag.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link from the 0x35-0x38 chunk type to a zlib path returning specifically Z_OK is inferred from switch-arm hit counts and source, not directly traced through the inflate return value.
